content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the diff against `origin/master` and read the milestone CLI/model changes end-to-end. Found 3 fix-now robustness issues, all centered on `milestone plan`; `create`, `show`, and `status` otherwise looked consistent with the existing milestone storage APIs.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `milestone plan` leaks the `__starting__:` reservation when setup fails before `draft_milestone()` runs, permanently wedging the milestone until `status.json` is edited by hand.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/cli/milestone.rs:567-570
- src/cli/milestone.rs:623-640
remediation_hint: Once the reservation has been written, clear it on every subsequent error path (`EffectiveConfig::load`, `build_requirements_service`, and any other pre-run setup failure), or replace the marker with a lease/timeout-backed reservation so stale startup markers self-heal.
   Rationale: I reproduced this with `RALPH_BURNING_BACKEND=definitely-invalid ralph-burning milestone plan ...`: the first call fails during config validation, leaves `pending_requirements_run_id` set to `__starting__:*`, and every retry then hits the `planning is already starting in another process` branch at lines 625-631.
2. finding_summary: Once `pending_requirements_run_id` points at a missing or unusable requirements run, `milestone plan` has no recovery path and will repeat the same failure forever.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/cli/milestone.rs:154-188
- src/cli/milestone.rs:190-221
- src/cli/milestone.rs:553-565
- src/cli/milestone.rs:678-695
remediation_hint: Treat missing/invalid/nonterminal pending runs as stale state: clear the pointer (or allow an explicit restart path) before returning, and add a regression test for an interrupted milestone requirements run left in `drafting`.
   Rationale: `Existing(run_id)` at lines 554-565 blindly reuses whatever the snapshot points to, but only the explicit `RequirementsStatus::Failed` branch clears the pointer. A run left in `drafting`, a missing run directory, or a completed run whose handoff/materialization fails all leave the stale ID in place, so the next `milestone plan` just re-reads the same broken run and fails again.
3. finding_summary: `milestone plan` starts a full milestone requirements run even when the milestone is already `running`, and only discovers the status conflict after the expensive planning pass finishes.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/cli/milestone.rs:145-152
- src/cli/milestone.rs:553-573
- src/cli/milestone.rs:618-640
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1377-1387
remediation_hint: Reject `milestone plan` up front when the snapshot status is `running` (before reserving or invoking the requirements service), or at minimum clear the pending run when materialization is rejected for this reason.
   Rationale: I reproduced this by marking a milestone `running` and invoking `milestone plan` with the stub backend: the command created a completed requirements run, then `materialize_bundle()` rejected it because the milestone was running, leaving `pending_requirements_run_id` populated with the completed run. In production backends this burns real model work and mutates state on a request that should fail immediately.
